mmlu.description: A benchmark designed to rigorously evaluate multi-modal reasoning with explicit thinking traces. It includes a high-difficulty dataset spanning diverse reasoning types with symbolic depth and multi-hop demands.

mmlu_pro.description: An enhanced dataset designed to extend the MMLU benchmark by integrating more challenging, reasoning-focused questions and expanding the choice set from four to ten options to reduce random guessing.

gpqa.bmt_title: GPQA: A Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A Benchmark
gpqa.description: GPQA is a challenging multiple-choice question answering dataset written by domain experts in biology, physics, and chemistry. It is designed to be difficult even for highly skilled non-experts with unrestricted access to the internet (Google-proof), while domain experts can answer them with high accuracy.

humaneval.specs: Python functions with docstrings and unit tests
humaneval.description: A benchmark for evaluating the functional correctness of code generated by Large Language Models. It consists of programming problems that include a function signature, docstring, body, and unit tests.

math.specs: Text (LaTeX formatted math problems and solutions)
math.description: A dataset of 12,500 challenging competition mathematics problems (e.g., AMC 10, AIME, Putnam) covering algebra, geometry, counting, number theory, and more. It measures advanced mathematical problem-solving ability.

gsm8k.description: A dataset of high-quality linguistically diverse grade school math word problems. It is designed to support the task of question answering on basic mathematical problems that require multi-step reasoning.

mmmu.specs: Video, Text (QA); Covers 6 professional disciplines (Art, Business, Science, Medicine, Humanities, Engineering).
mmmu.description: A massive multi-discipline multimodal video benchmark designed to evaluate the knowledge acquisition capabilities of LMMs from expert-level professional videos. It assesses models on their ability to perceive, comprehend, and adapt knowledge from videos across six disciplines.

swe_bench.specs: Python codebases, GitHub issues/PRs, Docker execution environment
swe_bench.description: A benchmark for evaluating large language models on real-world software engineering issues collected from GitHub. It tasks agents with resolving issues (bugs/features) in popular Python repositories by generating patches that pass new tests.

truthfulqa.specs: Text questions (Multiple choice and Generation), CSV
truthfulqa.description: A benchmark to measure whether language models generate truthful answers to questions, focusing on imitative falsehoods and misconceptions.

ifeval.specs: Text-based, verifiable instructions (e.g., word count constraints, format requirements)
ifeval.description: A benchmark designed to evaluate the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to follow verifiable instructions, focusing on objective metrics rather than subjective human evaluation.

aime.specs: Text modality; LaTeX format; Includes problem type and ground-truth answers
aime.description: A dataset containing questions from the AIME 2025 competition, created as part of the MathArena framework to evaluate LLMs on uncontaminated, newly released mathematical problems.

hellaswag.description: An adversarial dataset for commonsense natural language inference where models must select the best ending to a context, designed to be hard for state-of-the-art models.

arc.specs: Text, Long-form reports, Multi-step web search
arc.description: A comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate Deep Research Agents (DRAs) capable of autonomous information retrieval, multi-stage reasoning, and structured long-form report generation. It consists of PhD-level research tasks crafted by domain experts across 22 distinct fields.

bbh.description: A subset of the BIG-Bench suite, focusing on 23 challenging tasks where prior language models did not outperform the average human-rater. It emphasizes multi-step reasoning capabilities.

mbpp.specs: Python code, natural language descriptions, test cases
mbpp.description: A dataset containing entry-level programming problems, designed to be solvable by entry-level programmers. Each problem consists of a task description, code solution, and 3 automated test cases.

medqa.specs: Text (Question Answering), Multiple Choice (4 or 5 options)
medqa.description: A large-scale open-domain question answering dataset collected from professional medical board exams. The primary subset consists of questions from the United States Medical Licensing Examination (USMLE).

pubmedqa.description: A biomedical question answering dataset collected from PubMed abstracts. The task is to answer research questions with yes/no/maybe using the corresponding abstract context.

medmcqa.description: A large-scale multiple-choice question answering dataset covering 21 medical subjects, including a significant number of dermatology questions. It is designed to address real-world medical entrance exam questions.

mathvista.specs: Derived from 28 existing datasets and 3 new ones (IQTest, FunctionQA, PaperQA); covers geometry, charts, and function plots.
mathvista.description: A benchmark specifically designed to evaluate mathematical reasoning in visual contexts, combining diverse mathematical and visual tasks.

docvqa.description: A dataset for Visual Question Answering (VQA) on document images, containing questions defined on a wide variety of document types.

mt_bench.description: A set of 80 high-quality multi-turn questions designed to test the conversation flow and instruction-following capabilities of LLMs. It uses GPT-4 as a judge to evaluate model responses against a reference.

alpaca_eval.description: An automatic evaluator for instruction-following models that measures win rates against a reference model (e.g., GPT-4 Turbo) using an LLM-as-a-judge. It simplifies the AlpacaFarm dataset for faster and cheaper evaluation.

livecodebench.specs: Code generation, self-repair, code execution, test output prediction tasks; Problems from LeetCode, AtCoder, CodeForces
livecodebench.description: LiveCodeBench is a holistic and contamination-free evaluation benchmark for Large Language Models (LLMs) for code. It continuously collects new problems from competitive programming contests (LeetCode, AtCoder, CodeForces) to prevent test set contamination. The benchmark assesses models on a broader range of code-related capabilities beyond just generation, including self-repair, code execution, and test output prediction.

chatbot-arena-elo.specs: Text conversations, Pairwise preference votes, JSON
chatbot-arena-elo.description: An open platform for evaluating Large Language Models (LLMs) based on human preferences. It uses a crowdsourced, pairwise comparison approach where users chat with two anonymous models and vote on the better response, generating a leaderboard via the Bradley-Terry model.

webarena.specs: Web-based tasks, Text/HTML modality, Dockerized environments
webarena.description: A realistic and reproducible web environment for building and evaluating autonomous agents. It consists of fully functional websites from four common domains (e-commerce, social forums, software development, content management) to test agents on long-horizon web tasks.

gaia.specs: Text questions, varying modalities (images, files), Tool use required
gaia.description: A benchmark for General AI Assistants featuring conceptually simple questions for humans that are challenging for AI. It requires fundamental abilities such as reasoning, tool use, and multi-modality to solve real-world tasks.
